summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held its January 2021 meeting, electing officers and approving resolutions from prior cases. Ed was re-elected as chairperson and Bob as secretary. The board approved multiple resolutions from BZA cases heard in late 2020, including variances for property construction and one denial with conditions. Discussion also covered the zoning inspector vacancy, BZA member openings, meeting scheduling, and member compensation limits.
decisions: Resolution R-97-2020 approved: Variance for Wenda Taylor at 6342 Telfer Farm Lane for rear yard construction
Resolution approved: Variance for property at 1470 with landscaping conditions
Resolution R-003-2021 approved: Variance for 576 sq ft construction at 1565 Road
Resolution R-99-2020 approved: Denial of variance for Sean Ryan at 6472 Manila Road with conditions (buildings 1, 3, 4 approved; building 2 to be removed)
Resolution approved: Variance for CBDG Ocean LLC at 1910 State Route 28
action_items: Zoning staff to advertise openings for BZA member and two alternates
Applicant at 1470 required to install 30x10 streetscape landscape area with six 20-inch shrubs by April 30, 2021
Sean Ryan required to remove building 2 from property at 6472 Manila Road
Zoning staff to send email notifications about upcoming meetings and agenda items
Staff to continue work on accessory structures text amendment through zoning commission process
key_amounts: $599 per year compensation limit for BZA members
1500 hours per year / 28 hours per week for zoning inspector position
notable_topics: Election of BZA chairperson and secretary
Approval of multiple variance resolutions from 2020 cases
Denial resolution with conditions for Sean Ryan property
Zoning inspector vacancy and position details
BZA and zoning commission member recruitment
Meeting scheduling changes from Mondays to Tuesdays for January/February
BZA member compensation and overtime concerns
Case load management and legal time requirements for hearings
notes: Much of the meeting involved approving resolutions for cases previously heard in November and December 2020. Audio quality appears poor with many inaudible portions in the transcript. The board discussed concerns about the $599 annual compensation cap for members and the challenge of managing case loads within legal time requirements. The previous zoning inspector left on December 31st, a week early, and a replacement is being sought. The board also discussed the need for a vice chairperson but deferred due to not having bylaws available.
title: BZA 1192021
